violations.expected_code:
# Use GeminiRAHelper method to access edit page
GeminiRAHelper.access_subscription_edit_page(page)
violations.explanation: Inline page.locator() with hardcoded selector used in test function body during precondition setup. This violates Prohibition 6 which states: ZERO page.locator() in test function body. Admin/backend interactions should be encapsulated in helper methods, not raw inline page.locator() chains. This is also a violation of checkpoint D15 (admin interactions should be in helpers).

fix_details.replace_with: # Navigate to subscription edit - use helper method when available

violations.current_code:
"""C44873414 - Billing Cycle Period card

Verifies Billing Cycle Period card display and behavior on Print and Payment History page
when subscription plan is paused, including complimentary pages progress bar, additional
pages tracking, tooltips, and page usage information across different printing scenarios.
"""
violations.expected_code:
# At module level BEFORE imports:
"""Test Case C44873414 - Billing Cycle Period card

Verifies Billing Cycle Period card display and behavior on Print and Payment History page
when subscription plan is paused, including complimentary pages progress bar, additional
pages tracking, tooltips, and page usage information across different printing scenarios.

Test Case ID: C44873414
Module: HP Instant Ink
Priority: P2
"""
violations.explanation: The module-level docstring is present but appears after the function definition line in the provided code. According to coding standards, the module-level docstring must be at the very top of the file, BEFORE all import statements. The current placement suggests it may be inside the function, which would violate Prohibition 7 (no function-level docstrings).

fix_details.replace_with: # Move this docstring to the very top of the file, before all imports

violations.explanation: This expect() call DOES have an explicit timeout, so this is actually NOT a violation. However, I'm flagging it for review because to_be_hidden() assertions should verify element is not visible, and the framework pattern typically uses to_be_visible() with negation or checks visibility state differently.

fix_details.replace_with:
# Verify plan pause info is not displayed
plan_pause_count = print_history_page.plan_pause_info.count()
assert plan_pause_count == 0, f"Plan pause info should not be visible, found {plan_pause_count} elements"

violations.explanation: Admin/backend interactions (Rails Admin operations) are performed using inline page.locator() chains instead of being encapsulated in helper methods. This entire pause plan operation should be a single helper method call.

changes.code_to_add:
@staticmethod
def pause_subscription_via_rails_admin(page):
    """Pause subscription via Rails Admin interface."""
    page.locator("a:has-text('Edit')").first.click()
    page.wait_for_load_state('networkidle', timeout=30000)
    page.locator("select[name='subscription[subscription_state]']").select_option('paused')
    page.locator("input[type='submit'][value='Save']").click()
    page.wait_for_load_state('networkidle', timeout=30000)

fix_details.explanation: Create helper method in GeminiRAHelper to encapsulate the pause subscription operation, then use it in the test script

violations.expected_code:
from core.settings import framework_logger
from core.settings import GlobalState
violations.explanation: Import order is slightly non-standard. While importing multiple items from the same module on one line is acceptable, the framework pattern typically imports framework_logger alone on its own line, and GlobalState is imported separately only when needed. This is a minor style issue.

fix_details.replace_with:
from core.settings import framework_logger
from core.settings import GlobalState

violations.explanation: The code contains placeholder/TODO-style comments like 'when available' which suggest incomplete implementation. Production-ready code should not have such placeholders.

violations.current_code: # Precondition 4 has multiple log lines for a single operation
violations.expected_code: # Consolidate to 1-2 log lines per logical step
violations.explanation: The precondition 4 section has verbose logging with multiple framework_logger.info() calls for what is essentially a single operation (pausing the subscription). According to E04, logging should be concise with 1 log line per step. While preconditions can have more logging than test steps, this is still excessive.

fix_details.find:
framework_logger.info("Precondition: Pausing subscription plan")
GeminiRAHelper.access(page)
GeminiRAHelper.access_tenant_page(page, tenant_email)
# Navigate to subscription edit page
page.locator("a:has-text('Edit')").first.click()
page.wait_for_load_state('networkidle', timeout=30000)
# Find and select pause subscription option
page.locator("select[name='subscription[subscription_state]']").select_option('paused')
# Save changes
page.locator("input[type='submit'][value='Save']").click()
page.wait_for_load_state('networkidle', timeout=30000)
GeminiRAHelper.verify_rails_admin_info(page, "Subscription State", "paused", retry=True)
framework_logger.info("Subscription paused successfully")
fix_details.replace_with:
framework_logger.info("Precondition: Pausing subscription plan via Rails Admin")
GeminiRAHelper.access(page)
GeminiRAHelper.access_tenant_page(page, tenant_email)
GeminiRAHelper.pause_subscription_via_rails_admin(page)
GeminiRAHelper.verify_rails_admin_info(page, "Subscription State", "paused", retry=True)
framework_logger.info("Subscription paused successfully")

violations.expected_code:
# In merge instructions, show only the attribute assignments:
plan_pause_info = "[data-testid='plan-pause-info']"
billing_cycle_period_card = "[data-testid='billing-cycle-period-card']"
violations.explanation: The page additions file defines a standalone Elements class when it should only provide the string selector attributes to be added to the existing Elements class in the target file. The file structure is incorrect - it should show merge instructions with just the attributes, not a complete class definition. This makes it harder to merge and could cause confusion.

fix_details.description: Remove the 'class Elements:' line and just show the attribute assignments that should be added to the existing Elements class

violations.expected_code:
# In __init__ section of merge instructions:
self.plan_pause_info = page.locator(self.elements.plan_pause_info)
self.billing_cycle_period_card = page.locator(self.elements.billing_cycle_period_card)
violations.explanation: The locator initialization pattern is correct (using page.locator(self.elements.X)), but the file structure makes it appear as if these are standalone statements rather than merge instructions. The file should clearly indicate these go in the __init__ method of the target class. The current format is acceptable but could be clearer.

The tooltip locator uses a compound selector with data-testid as primary and role='tooltip' as fallback. While this is a good defensive pattern, the comment indicates 'Fallback to role if data-testid not available', suggesting uncertainty about the actual selector. In production code, the correct selector should be verified and the fallback removed if not needed. This is a minor issue as the pattern will work, but it's not ideal.

fix_details.find: complimentary_pages_tooltip = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-tooltip'], [role='tooltip']"  # Fallback to role if data-testid not available
fix_details.replace_with: complimentary_pages_tooltip = "[role='tooltip']"  # Tooltip appears on hover/click of info icon

violations.explanation: Same issue as V014 - compound selector with uncertain primary selector. Should be simplified to the working selector after verification.

fix_details.find: additional_pages_tooltip = "[data-testid='additional-pages-tooltip'], [role='tooltip']"  # Fallback to role if data-testid not available
fix_details.replace_with: additional_pages_tooltip = "[role='tooltip']"  # Tooltip appears on hover/click of info icon

fix_priority_order.fix_approach: Replace all inline page.locator() calls in the precondition 4 section with a single GeminiRAHelper method call. Create a new helper method pause_subscription_via_rails_admin() in GeminiRAHelper that encapsulates the entire pause operation (navigate to edit, select paused state, save). This eliminates 3 CRITICAL violations and also resolves the inline_admin_interaction violation (V006).
This is resolved by fixing priority 1 violations. The entire admin interaction block should be replaced with a single helper method call.

Restructure the page additions file to remove the standalone 'class Elements:' definition and show only the attribute assignments as merge instructions. The file should clearly indicate these are attributes to ADD to the existing Elements class, not a new class definition.
Move the module-level docstring to the very top of the file, before all import statements. Ensure it includes Test Case ID, Module, and Priority information.
Simplify tooltip locators to use only [role='tooltip'] selector after verifying it works correctly. Remove the compound selector pattern with uncertain data-testid primary selector.
Consolidate logging in precondition 4 to fewer, more concise log statements. This will be automatically resolved when the helper method is created (priority 1).
Remove placeholder comments like 'when available' that suggest incomplete implementation.
Split the combined import of framework_logger and GlobalState into two separate import lines for consistency with framework patterns.
